title: Node.js Backend Basics
description: Stand up an HTTP server with the built-in `node:http` module, then layer routing, JSON parsing, and file persistence on top. No frameworks — you'll learn what Express is hiding from you.

lessons.title: Your first http.createServer
lessons.content: The request is a stream. The response is a stream. Streams are not magic.

lessons.title: Routing by method and path
lessons.content: Switch on req.method and req.url. That's it.

lessons.title: Parsing JSON bodies
lessons.content: Buffer the chunks, JSON.parse the buffer, validate the shape, then act.

lessons.title: Persisting to disk
lessons.content: fs.writeFileSync is fine for a course. Real systems use a database.
